note: Build-ready vertical slice definitions. No DB schema, no API endpoints, no UI. Each slice is implementable by one developer without ambiguity. Architect corrections C1 and C2 from 070 review applied.

architect_corrections_applied.rationale: State mutation with invariant enforcement is domain protection, not coordination/routing. Misclassification would cause it to become lim-kode in build phase.

architect_corrections_applied.to_080: BS-ADDR-01 ResolveAddresses — single slice (adapter routing internal)
architect_corrections_applied.rationale: Lookup and routing are one execution flow. Keeping them as separate slices risks splitting the logic incorrectly and creating 'half logic' in two handlers.

slices.name: ComposeBroadcast
slices.handler_responsibility: Create a new Broadcast entity in DRAFT state under a validated ProfileId. Apply ProfileRoleAssignment gate to enforce role-driven field defaults (sendToOwner, channels, lookupConfig). Create channel-specific content extensions (BroadcastSmsContent, BroadcastEmailContent) when channels are declared. Return BroadcastId. No lookup or dispatch.

input.name: string (required)
input.lookupConfig: LookupConfig value object (sendToOwner, sendToAddress, lookupPrivate, lookupBusiness, ownerAddressType)
input.smsContent: BroadcastSmsContent input (optional — required when channels includes SMS)
input.emailContent: BroadcastEmailContent input (optional — required when channels includes Email)

invariants_enforced: BR024 — AlwaysOwner role → override lookupConfig.sendToOwner=true regardless of input
BR025 — DontSendEmail role → override channels.email=false regardless of input
BR026 — CanSpecifyLookup role → BLOCK sendToOwner/sendToAddress input if absent
BR027 — CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate → BLOCK lookupPrivate/lookupBusiness input if absent
ProfileId must be active (Customer aggregate gate via in-process call to EvaluateProfileGate)
side_effects: BroadcastSmsContent created if SMS channel declared
BroadcastEmailContent created if Email channel declared
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Not idempotent. Caller provides client-side idempotency key if needed. Concurrent creates are independent Broadcasts.

slices.name: AddRecipientCriterion
slices.handler_responsibility: Append one RecipientCriterion to an existing Broadcast in DRAFT state. Reject if Broadcast is locked (active or isLookedUp). Validate that at least one of phone, address criterion, or standardReceiverId is present.
invariants_enforced: BR001 — reject if Broadcast.active=true
BR002 — reject if Broadcast.isLookedUp=true
At least one of phone / address / standardReceiverId must be set
If standardReceiverId provided: verify existence in AGG-SUB before accepting
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Concurrent adds safe. Guard prevents add after activation. No natural idempotency key — duplicate adds create duplicate criteria.

slices.name: ActivateBroadcast
slices.handler_responsibility: Transition Broadcast from DRAFT to ACTIVATED. Re-evaluate ProfileGate. Validate recipients exist. Apply routing decision (sync/async/last-minute-schedule) and delegate to the appropriate dispatch slice. IDEMPOTENT: if already active, return existing BroadcastId with no side effects.
invariants_enforced: BR004 — idempotent: already active → early return, no side effects
BR005 — zero resolvable recipients → reject with NoRecipientsException
BR006 — >25000 Kvhx criteria → emit large-broadcast warning (non-blocking)
BR007 — scheduledSendAt set → ALWAYS route async. Never sync.
BR008 — sync gate: ByAddress + fromApi=true + ≤5 items + no zip-only → sync path
R037 — lastMinuteLookup=true AND scheduledSendAt set → skip lookup, queue ScheduledLookupTrigger 5 min before send
side_effects: One of: dispatches BS-MSG-04 (sync) / enqueues BS-MSG-05 (async) / queues ScheduledLookupTrigger (R037)

routing_table.sync: BR008=true AND BR007=false AND R037=false → BS-MSG-04
routing_table.last_minute: R037=true AND scheduledSendAt set → ScheduledLookupTrigger queued → BS-MSG-06 fires 5 min before send

slices.concurrency_idempotency: IDEMPOTENT (BR004). Concurrent activations safe — second call exits early. Routing happens at most once.

slices.name: ExecuteSynchronousDispatch
slices.handler_responsibility: Resolve all RecipientCriteria to phone numbers (via BS-ADDR-01 ResolveAddresses), create Message records with merge-applied text, dispatch to gateway synchronously, set isLookedUp=true BEFORE dispatch. Non-SMS channel content (email, voice) dispatched via respective channel handlers — marked as sms_derived_extension.
invariants_enforced: SC001 guard — isLookedUp=true written BEFORE gateway dispatch. Crash between write and dispatch → Messages exist with no-send status. StatusProgressionGuard handles.
BR008 precondition — gate must be true before entry (enforced by BS-MSG-03 routing)
StatusProgressionGuard active from first Message creation
side_effects: Messages bulk-created with resolved phone numbers and merge-applied text
Messages dispatched to GatewayApi or Strex (per gateway selection)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: NOT idempotent. Protected by BS-MSG-03 idempotency + isLookedUp=true gate. If isLookedUp already true: abort.
slices.domain_scope_note: sms_derived_extension: BroadcastEmailContent dispatched via Email channel handler in same orchestration. Not promoted to shared messaging candidate without separate architectural decision (PN-001).

slices.name: EnqueueAsyncDispatch
slices.handler_responsibility: Enqueue BroadcastId to async lookup pipeline (Azure Service Bus or equivalent). Does not perform lookup or dispatch — those are handled by BS-MSG-06 when dequeued.
invariants_enforced: Broadcast.active=true must be true before enqueue (enforced by BS-MSG-03 routing)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Safe to enqueue. Downstream BS-MSG-06 guards against duplicate processing via isLookedUp gate.

slices.name: ExecuteAsyncLookupAndDispatch
slices.handler_responsibility: Dequeued handler: resolve all RecipientCriteria to phone numbers (via BS-ADDR-01 ResolveAddresses), bulk-create Messages, bulk-create UnresolvedMessageRecords for no-phone addresses, write isLookedUp=true before gateway dispatch, dispatch to gateway in bulk. SHORT-CIRCUIT if isLookedUp already true.
invariants_enforced: SC001 guard — SHORT-CIRCUIT and log if Broadcast.isLookedUp=true on entry
isLookedUp=true written atomically BEFORE gateway dispatch
BR006 warning at >25000 Kvhx (non-blocking)
Bulk insert batch size: 300 (B018)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: SC001: isLookedUp=true = primary race guard. Duplicate dequeue → noop after SHORT-CIRCUIT check.

slices.name: ReceiveDeliveryStatusCallback
slices.handler_responsibility: Accept gateway DLR callback. Parse MessageId as LONG (B014 — GatewayApi truncation bug). Resolve Message. Create DeliveryStatusEvent (imported=false) via IDeliveryStatusWriter. Acknowledge callback to gateway immediately — progression processing is async (BS-MSG-08).

input.messageId: long (MUST parse as long — not int — B014 guard)

invariants_enforced: B014 guard — MessageId MUST be parsed as long. Int parse silently truncates IDs > 2.1B.
R036 — write via IDeliveryStatusWriter ONLY. No direct table access.
Idempotency guard on (messageId + gatewayReference + rawStatusCode): duplicate callback → return existing event ID, no new record
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Gateways retry callbacks. Idempotency guard by (messageId + gatewayReference + rawStatusCode). Duplicate creates are safe — BS-MSG-08 handles ordering.
slices.cross_aggregate_contract: IDeliveryStatusWriter (Messaging owns semantics — Email/Voice/Eboks channels call the same contract — no shared ownership of storage, CAC-001 / PN-002)

slices.name: ProcessStatusProgression
slices.architect_correction: C1 — reclassified from orchestration to command. This is domain-protection with invariant enforcement, not coordination/routing.
slices.handler_responsibility: Process a batch of pending DeliveryStatusEvents (imported=false). For each event: compare incoming status rank against current Message.statusCode rank. If higher: update Message.statusCode + dateStatusUpdatedAt. Always: set DeliveryStatusEvent.imported=true. Terminal statuses block all further statusCode updates. StatusProgressionGuard is the invariant — NOT rowversion.
invariants_enforced: R035 + StatusProgressionGuard — statusCode only increases in rank. Compare MUST be atomic: read current rank + compare + update in single transaction.
Terminal statuses (DELIVERED, FAILED_FINAL) are immutable — any callback against a terminal Message is rejected (imported=true, no statusCode change)
StatusProgressionGuard is the controlling invariant — NOT rowversion, NOT optimistic locking at the DB level (architect decision from 040)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: IDEMPOTENT per event after first processing (imported=true → skip). StatusProgressionGuard compare must execute within a transaction to prevent concurrent rank-writes from both winning. Batch processing is serializable per Message.

slices.name: QueryBroadcastStatus
slices.handler_responsibility: Return status summary for a Broadcast: recipient count, sent count, delivered count, failed count. Optionally return per-message status breakdown. Reads Message aggregate state — no mutations.
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: ManageCustomerHierarchy
slices.handler_responsibility: Create or update a Customer entity with its CustomerAccount. Create a Profile under the Customer. Update existing Profile properties. Deactivate Customer or Profile (soft delete). Conversion-specific: accept customerId assignment from PL-PIPE-02 (Prospect conversion PATCH 1).

input.operation: enum: CreateCustomer | UpdateCustomer | CreateProfile | UpdateProfile | DeactivateCustomer | DeactivateProfile

invariants_enforced: Customer must be active before Profile creation
Profile names unique within Customer (non-blocking warning, not hard reject)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Create not idempotent. Update idempotent by entity ID.

slices.name: EvaluateProfileGate
slices.handler_responsibility: Evaluate all ProfileRoleAssignments for a ProfileId. Return the effective permission set (EffectiveProfilePermissions). Called by BS-MSG-01 (compose) and BS-MSG-03 (activate) — NOT by the end user. Pure projection, no mutations.
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: ManageApiKeyAccess
slices.handler_responsibility: Create or revoke CustomerApiKey. On create: generate random key, hash it, persist hash only, return cleartext once. On revoke: set isActive=false. Cleartext is never retrievable after creation.

input.permissions: flags: ApiSendSingle | ApiHandleRecipients | ApiHandleMessageTemplates (required for Create)

output.cleartextKey: string (CREATE only — not stored, returned once)

invariants_enforced: SECURITY OWASP A02 — cleartext NEVER persisted. Hash must be non-reversible (bcrypt or SHA-256+salt).
Cleartext returned to caller exactly once — not stored, not retrievable.
Revoke is idempotent — already-revoked key returns success
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Create not idempotent. Revoke idempotent.

slices.name: ManageStandardReceiver
slices.handler_responsibility: Create, update, or deactivate a StandardReceiver and its associated groups, keywords, distribution phones, and Profile mappings. Enforce keyword uniqueness per country. Validate ProfileId existence before creating mapping.

input.operation: enum: CreateReceiver | UpdateReceiver | DeactivateReceiver | AddGroup | AddKeyword | AddDistributionPhone | MapToProfile

invariants_enforced: Keyword must be unique per country — reject with KeywordConflictException if duplicate
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Keyword create fails on duplicate (not idempotent). Other creates not idempotent. Updates idempotent by ID.

slices.name: ManageReceiverPricing
slices.handler_responsibility: Upsert a ReceiverPriceSetting for a (customerId, standardReceiverId, countryId) composite key. If no override exists, create. If override exists, update. Fully idempotent.
slices.concurrency_idempotency: IDEMPOTENT — upsert by composite key. Last writer wins.

slices.name: PrepareImportJob
slices.handler_responsibility: Accept an uploaded file (blob reference) and create an ImportJob. Parse column headers. Create ImportColumnMappings (optionally pre-filled from ImportSavedConfiguration). Transition status machine from Queued→ProcessingColumns→ColumnsReady→ReadyForImport. Record sizeKb — routing to inline vs Azure Batch determined at confirm time (R042).
invariants_enforced: Status machine: Queued(1) → ProcessingColumns(2) → ColumnsReady(3) → ReadyForImport(4). Must advance in order.
If column detection fails → status=Error(-1) immediately
slices.concurrency_idempotency: One job per file upload. Not idempotent (each call = new ImportJob).

slices.name: ConfirmImportJob
slices.handler_responsibility: Execute the two-phase import commit. PHASE 1: validate ImportRows against column mappings. PHASE 2: write CommitStarted(107) marker atomically, then commit valid rows to target aggregate. Route inline vs Azure Batch based on sizeKb (R042). Transition to DataImported(6) on success, Error(-1) on failure. If status=CommitStarted on entry → hand off to BS-IMP-03 ResumeImportJob.

output.status: ImportStatus (DataImported=6 | Error=-1 | CommitStarted=107 if routed to Batch)

invariants_enforced: R041 — CommitStarted(107) MUST be written BEFORE first data row committed. Atomic write: if CommitStarted fails, abort. Never commit data without marker.
R042 — sizeKb < 10 → inline commit; sizeKb >= 10 → dispatch to Azure Batch (idempotent resume via BS-IMP-03)
Only valid ImportRows committed to target aggregate
Terminal state guard — DataImported(6) or Error(-1) on entry → reject with AlreadyProcessedException
side_effects: Valid ImportRows committed to target aggregate (e.g. AGG-SUB for subscriber data)
ImportJob.status = DataImported(6) on success OR Error(-1) on failure
slices.concurrency_idempotency: CommitStarted(107) enables crash-safe resume via BS-IMP-03. Terminal state check prevents re-run. Azure Batch path: handler exits after enqueue, BS-IMP-03 handles completion.

slices.name: ResumeImportJob
slices.handler_responsibility: Resume an ImportJob from CommitStarted(107) state after crash or Azure Batch worker restart. Read last-committed row offset from ImportRows. Continue committing remaining valid rows from that offset. Complete to DataImported(6) or Error(-1).

input.importJobId: identifier (required — must be in CommitStarted=107 status)

invariants_enforced: R041 — entry condition: status MUST be CommitStarted(107). Reject other statuses.
Resume reads last row offset — does not re-commit already-committed rows (idempotency)
side_effects: Remaining valid rows committed to target aggregate
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Designed for single concurrent execution per importJobId. Multiple resumes are safe — committed-row-offset prevents double-commit.

slices.name: ResolveAddresses
slices.architect_correction: C2 — ExecuteAddressLookup + RouteToCountryAdapter merged. Resolution and routing are one execution flow. Adapter selection is internal to this slice.
slices.handler_responsibility: Resolve a list of RecipientCriteria to (Kvhx, PhoneNumber?, OwnerName?) tuples. Internally: route each criterion to the correct IAddressAdapter (by countryId). Adapter resolves country-native key to Kvhx. Core receives only Kvhx. Fetch AddressOwner data when Broadcast.sendToOwner=true. Return unresolvable criteria as empty results for UnresolvedMessageRecord creation by caller.

input.sendToOwner: bool (if true: fetch AddressOwner for each resolved Kvhx)

output.resolved: ResolvedCriterion[] (each: criterionId, kvhx, phone?, ownerName?, ownerType?)

invariants_enforced: R043 — IAddressAdapter per country. Core only receives Kvhx. Country-native keys stay within adapter.
R023 — Kvhx string is canonical output. Never replaced by DB surrogate.
FI (Finland) — route to NullAdapter / throw NotSupportedException until source replaced (ADDR-E07 DEFERRED)
canonical_state_only — reads ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress from canonical cache. Does NOT read staging tables (ADDR-E05 NorwegianAddress, ADDR-E06 SwedishAddress) — those are adapter-internal.
slices.concurrency_idempotency: IDEMPOTENT — reads canonical state. Deterministic output for same input given same canonical cache state.

slices.name: LookupAddressOwner
slices.handler_responsibility: Return owner details (name, phone, ownerType) for a given Kvhx. Reads from canonical ADDR-E02 AddressOwner cache only. Called only when sendToOwner=true. Can be called standalone or invoked internally by BS-ADDR-01.
invariants_enforced: Reads canonical ADDR-E02 AddressOwner only. Not country adapter staging tables (PN-003).
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Read-only. No concurrency concerns.

slices.name: RunCountryAddressImport
slices.handler_responsibility: Import or refresh address data from a country source (Kartverket/NO, Lantmäteriet/SE, Datafordeleren/DK). Adapter-internal: parse source data → update country staging tables (ADDR-E05/E06) → resolve Kvhx for each entry → write/update canonical ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress + ADDR-E02 AddressOwner → detect and record AddressKvhxChanges. The only output visible to Messaging aggregate is updated canonical ADDR-E01 state.
invariants_enforced: R043 — country-native key resolved to Kvhx by adapter before writing canonical state
FI DEFERRED — reject with NotSupportedException for countryId=FI
Staging tables (ADDR-E05, ADDR-E06) are adapter-internal — not exposed via output or query
side_effects: ADDR-E01 ResolvedAddress refreshed (canonical state — visible to Messaging)
ADDR-E03 AddressKvhxChange records created for detected renames
Country adapter staging tables updated (adapter-internal — not visible outside BS-ADDR-03)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Idempotent per source batch reference. Re-importing same batch: canonical state retains last-write, Kvhx renames re-detected (no duplicates — upsert by Kvhx).

slices.name: ManageProspectLifecycle
slices.handler_responsibility: Create a new Prospect or advance its status through the ProspectStatus FSM. Add and update contact persons, product configurations, and user role pre-configurations (ProspectUserRoleMapping). No Customer creation — that only happens in BS-PIPE-02.

input.operation: enum: CreateProspect | UpdateProspect | AddContactPerson | UpdateContactPerson | AddProduct | UpdateProduct | AddUserRoleMapping | AdvanceStatus
input.name: string (required for Create)

invariants_enforced: Status transitions must respect ProspectStatus FSM (no backward transitions)
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Create not idempotent. Updates and status transitions idempotent by entity ID.

slices.name: ConvertProspectToCustomer
slices.handler_responsibility: Execute two-step non-atomic Prospect→Customer conversion. PATCH 1: create or link a Customer entity in AGG-CUST; set Prospect.customerId; COPY ProspectContactPersons as new Customer ContactPersons. PATCH 2: DESTRUCTIVELY REPLACE CustomerProducts from ProspectProducts (delete existing first, then create from Prospect). ProcessTasks STAY on Prospect (R039). ProspectUserRoleMappings NOT activated (R040).

input.customerId: CustomerId? (optional for Patch1 — if linking existing Customer)

invariants_enforced: R038 — two-step non-atomic. Each patch is a separate command invocation.
R039 — ProcessTasks NOT transferred. Handler MUST NOT migrate tasks. Absence is correct behavior.
R040 — ProspectUserRoleMappings NOT activated. Handler MUST NOT apply them to Customer. Absence is correct behavior.
Patch1 idempotent: if Prospect.customerId already set, return existing customerId.
Patch2 guard: Prospect.customerId must be set before Patch2 is accepted.
Patch2 destructive guard: log CustomerProduct delete count before applying (audit trail)
side_effects: PATCH 1: Prospect.customerId set. ContactPersons COPIED to Customer (originals remain on Prospect).
PATCH 2: All existing CustomerProducts for customerId DELETED. New CustomerProducts created from Prospect.ProspectProducts.
slices.concurrency_idempotency: PATCH 1: idempotent by customerId already set. PATCH 2: NOT idempotent end-to-end (destructive delete+create). Guard: check Prospect status before Patch2 to prevent double-apply.

slices.name: ManageProcessTask
slices.handler_responsibility: Create, update, or complete a ProcessTask under a polymorphic owner (Prospect or Customer). Validate ownerType against ProcessTaskTemplate.allowedOwnerTypes (application-layer, no DB FK). Validate ownerId references a real entity of the declared ownerType.

input.ownerId: identifier (required for Create — resolved against ownerType)

invariants_enforced: R039 — ownerType must be in ProcessTaskTemplate.allowedOwnerTypes. Reject with InvalidOwnerTypeException if not.
ownerId must reference a valid entity of the declared ownerType (application-layer check)
CompleteTask is idempotent — already-completed task returns success without re-applying
slices.concurrency_idempotency: Create not idempotent. Complete idempotent. Concurrent updates: last writer wins (no optimistic locking at this level).

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: BroadcastActivation → AddressResolution
cross_aggregate_coordination.contract: IAddressAdapter — input: RecipientCriterion[] + countryId → output: ResolvedCriterion[] (Kvhx only)
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R043 / CAC-004 — Kvhx string is the only data crossing AGG-MSG / AGG-ADDR boundary. No country-native keys in Messaging.

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: DeliveryStatus Cross-Channel Write
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R036 / CAC-001 / PN-002 — Messaging owns write-surface semantics. Other channels hold contract access only — not shared aggregate ownership.

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: BroadcastCompose/Activate → ProfileGate
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: CAC-003 ProfileGate — checked at both compose (BS-MSG-01) and activate (BS-MSG-03). Read-only cross-aggregate call.

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: ImportConfirm → Target Aggregate Write
cross_aggregate_coordination.callee: Target aggregate command (e.g. BS-SUB-01 ManageStandardReceiver for subscriber imports)
cross_aggregate_coordination.contract: Typed domain command per import type
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: Import aggregate owns import lifecycle only. Target data ownership stays in target aggregate.

cross_aggregate_coordination.name: ProspectConversion → Customer Create
cross_aggregate_coordination.boundary_rule: R038 / CAC-005 — two-step non-atomic. PATCH 1 only. No implicit cascade for tasks (R039) or role activation (R040).
